original_prompt:
PORTFOLIO_CHILD_PROJECT: true
Parent portfolio: batch-cat-b2
Selected app slug: interval

Build this app as a working local-first SwiftUI MVP.

## Selected App Spec

# Interval

Build mode: **MVP build**.

## App Name

Interval

## Category

Productivity

## One-Sentence Promise

A focus timer that learns your real focus patterns and adapts session length/timing to you

## Claude Design Handoff Prompt

(not supplied yet)

## Parent Portfolio Prompt

Build 6 completely separate production-ready iOS apps at the same time: exactly 3 Photo & Video apps and 3 Productivity apps.

This is a multi-app PORTFOLIO request: multiple apps in one program. Each selected app becomes its own separate project — one folder per app.


Requirements for every app:
1. Must go from 0 to production-ready, not just a few features.
2. Must be unique, useful, and commercially viable.
3. Must be beautifully designed and feel premium.
4. Must provide real end-user value.
5. Must have a realistic path to monetization — a subscription or paid offering with genuine value (never cosmetics-only).
6. Should have viral potential (broad or niche) without sacrificing usefulness.
7. Must be better than its competitors in a meaningful way.
8. Local-first where applicable, architected so cloud support can be added later without rewriting.

UNIQUENESS (hard rule, non-negotiable):
- No app in this batch may be similar to another app in this batch.
- No app may be similar to anything already built or being built in this workspace or before, including: Waylay (location-based personal recall utility), TrueScale, Provenance, Scope-Guard, Practice-Loop, Proof, ReturnWise, VerveCoach, CueKeeper, Countertop, FieldQuote, my existing digital temple app, and my existing PO-automation tool.
- No app may be similar to apps in the OTHER batches of this program (check sibling project folders in the workspace for their concepts).
- Do not reuse prior concepts, themes, or mechanics from earlier apps — even if the request sounds similar to something built before, produce a differentiated concept.

Bonus points (optional — only when integral and genuinely valuable, never forced): unique use of LLMs, AR, or ML.

Build rules:
- Run the app efforts in parallel; keep discussion/design phases separate per app.
- Each app gets its own full product direction, design direction, architecture, implementation plan, and build output.
- Save all phase discussions and final decisions; at the end combine each app's full transcript into a .txt file.
- If an app is liked, also prepare Jira board + Notion project structures for backfilling later.
- Use enough rounds per phase for high-quality discussion, ending early on consensus.
- Recover cleanly from stalls; report only important milestones.

Output:
- One folder per app in the output directory — never one folder containing all apps.
Output directory:
- /Users/pchordia/Documents/iOS-App-Factory

Now generate at least 12 candidate ideas, discuss them thoroughly, choose the best 6, and build all 6 to production quality.


## Change requested
The app currently FAILS to compile. Fix every compiler error until the build succeeds cleanly; do not drop features unless unavoidable.

phase_discussions.title: Scope the change
phase_discussions.output: You've hit your monthly spend limit · raise it at claude.ai/settings/usage

phase_outputs.final_review:
You've hit your monthly spend limit · raise it at claude.ai/settings/usage

VERIFICATION: FAILED

tasks.title: Domain enums + single constants file
acceptance_criteria: SessionOutcome, LearningState, OutcomeProvenance, TimeBucket, SubscriptionTier, SessionEndTrigger all defined per tech_specs signatures

All tunables (10-90min focus clamp, 3-20min break clamp, 10%/5min step cap, 60s degenerate floor, 5-session adapted threshold, 20-session/unbounded rolling window) defined exactly once in Constants.swift
IntervalCore package builds with zero SwiftUI/StoreKit/UserNotifications imports anywhere in the target

tasks.title: SessionSnapshot, EvidenceMark, Recommendation value types
acceptance_criteria: SessionSnapshot is Codable/Sendable and is the only type RecommendationEngine will accept
Recommendation includes explanation, evidence, progressTowardAdaptation per tech_specs signature

tasks.title: SwiftData models: SessionRecord, Profile, AppSettingsRecord
acceptance_criteria: Fields match tech_specs SessionRecord signature exactly, including provenance and timeBucket stored (not recomputed)
No task-name/notes/journaling fields present

tasks.title: SessionRepository protocol + SwiftData implementation
acceptance_criteria: Concrete impl is MainActor-bound
deleteAllData wipes SessionRecord/Profile/learning state across all profiles but never touches AppSettingsRecord onboarding/permission flags
storeUnavailable error path is distinguishable from saveFailed/notFound

tasks.title: RecommendationEngine (pure)
acceptance_criteria: Foundation-only, no SwiftData/SwiftUI imports
Applies clamps, step cap, degenerate floor, rolling window, and 5-session adapted threshold sourced only from Constants.swift

tasks.title: ReconciliationPolicy (trigger-aware)
acceptance_criteria: provisionalOutcome(trigger:) never resolves manualStop to expiredWhileAway
appRelaunchReconciliation uses the >=90% elapsed threshold from Constants.swift
all elapsed-time math uses raw Date/TimeInterval arithmetic, never Calendar component subtraction

tasks.title: SessionRecord <-> SessionSnapshot translation + integration test
acceptance_criteria: Test builds a real SwiftData SessionRecord, translates it, runs it through RecommendationEngine, asserts output
Time bucket is read from stored value, never recomputed from raw timestamp

tasks.title: IntervalCore pure-logic test suite
acceptance_criteria: Synthetic [SessionSnapshot] arrays only, zero ModelContext setup
Covers every clamp/step-cap/degenerate-floor/threshold boundary and all three SessionEndTrigger cases

tasks.title: Repository + bootstrap + migration tests
acceptance_criteria: Deliberately corrupts the on-disk store (main file + WAL/SHM) and asserts clean recovery via AppLaunchBootstrap
One VersionedSchema/SchemaMigrationPlan round-trip test passes
deleteAllData test asserts all profiles wiped, AppSettingsRecord flags untouched

tasks.title: Clock protocol + NotificationKind enum
acceptance_criteria: Clock is injectable into SessionCoordinator for deterministic test timing

tasks.title: NotificationScheduler protocol + real UNUserNotificationCenter impl
acceptance_criteria: cancel(sessionID:) removes only that session's pending requests, never removeAllPendingNotificationRequests()
requestAuthorizationIfNeeded checks current settings first and never re-prompts after a prior denial

tasks.title: EntitlementState enum + SubscriptionStore (StoreKit 2)
acceptance_criteria: lapsed state preserves data access as read-only, never triggers deletion
purchase/restore/refreshEntitlements implemented against real StoreKit 2 Product/Transaction APIs

tasks.title: Notification + subscription test suites
acceptance_criteria: Notification tests assert zero pending requests for a session ID after cancel, and no re-prompt after simulated denial
SubscriptionStoreTests use StoreKitTest/SKTestSession to cover purchase, restore, restore-with-nothing, and every documented failure type

tasks.title: AppLaunchBootstrap (corrupted-store recovery)
acceptance_criteria: Catches ModelContainer init failure, deletes store file AND its WAL/SHM companions as one atomic set, reinitializes empty
Sets AppSettingsRecord.storeWasReset for a one-time honest message

tasks.title: SessionPhase enum
acceptance_criteria: Matches tech_specs signature exactly (idle/running/onBreak/awaitingOutcome/reconciliationPending)
This file plus SessionCoordinator's public API are tagged core-v1-frozen once T-016 passes T-017's tests, gating all further UI work

tasks.title: SessionCoordinator
acceptance_criteria: Sole writer of SessionPhase; timer truth is always startTimestamp+plannedDuration recomputed from Date()
stop()/naturalCompletion write a provisional outcome via ReconciliationPolicy before any sheet appears
reconcileOnLaunch() uses .appRelaunchReconciliation and never blocks starting a new session

tasks.title: SessionCoordinator on-device scenario matrix
acceptance_criteria: Real-device (not simulator-only) pass of: start->lock->elapse-past-end->relaunch; start->stop-at-40s; outcome-sheet-open->force-quit->relaunch; break transition; notification fires and cancels by correct UUID

tasks.title: Home screen + evidence strip
acceptance_criteria: Renders as a pure function of SessionPhase/Recommendation, no independent local state
Fresh install shows fixed 25/5 defaults with zero personalization language
Evidence strip rendered via Canvas with one summarized VoiceOver label

tasks.title: Active session screen + start-adjustment + outcome sheets
acceptance_criteria: Active session bound via fullScreenCover(item:), never NavigationStack push
Outcome sheet is pre-filled and swipe-dismissible when a provisional outcome already exists
Stop control requires confirmation and has deliberate spacing from Skip-break

tasks.title: History screen + HistoryViewModel
acceptance_criteria: No @Query used against SessionRecord anywhere in this screen; all reads go through HistoryViewModel/SessionRepository
This week strip shows real free-tier data; locked teaser row opens paywall
All/Completed/Early-stop/Interrupted filter implemented

tasks.title: Settings screen
acceptance_criteria: Upgrade row, notification status, precise local-storage privacy copy, delete-all-data row, subscription status incl. lapsed all present

tasks.title: RootView + app entry + TabView wiring
acceptance_criteria: Three tabs only: Home, History, Settings
RootView reads AppSettingsRecord.storeWasReset once on first appearance and clears it after showing the honest message
SessionCoordinator injected once via .environment(_:) at app root

tasks.title: Shared empty/error state components + Reduce Motion helper
acceptance_criteria: Shared across History, Settings, and the store-reset screen instead of bespoke per-screen states
Every .animation() call site in the app routes through ReduceMotionAnimator, returning nil when Reduce Motion is on

tasks.title: Paywall sheet + live preview
acceptance_criteria: default/purchasing/success/failed-by-type/restore states all implemented
makePaywallPreviewRecommendation uses the user's own real data when available, view-layer entitlement gate decides visibility, never a stock sample

tasks.title: Delete-all-data confirmation sheet
acceptance_criteria: States exactly what's erased and that subscription status is untouched
Destructive-styled confirm requiring explicit tap; brief success toast on return to Settings

tasks.title: Zero-network structural audit
acceptance_criteria: verifyNoNetworkInCorePath mechanically asserts zero networking types reachable from the timer/logging/adaptation module boundary

tasks.title: Accessibility pass across Home/Session/History
acceptance_criteria: VoiceOver milestone-only announcements (start/halfway/one-minute/complete), never per-second
Dynamic Type headroom verified on the why-line string at largest sizes
Outcome buttons reflow vertically at large Dynamic Type

tasks.title: Seeded debug dataset proving visible recommendation movement
acceptance_criteria: A seeded synthetic history visibly changes Home's on-screen recommendation and one-line why, not just an internal test value

tasks.title: Final integration hardening & go/no-go review
acceptance_criteria: Full scripted end-to-end QA matrix passes on-device with realistic interruption patterns
Cross-boundary repair pass complete (coordinator/repository races, store-reset messaging, accessibility regressions from final tweaks)
Go/no-go review confirms: engine tests pass, corruption recovery exercised, background/lock/kill reconciliation proven on-device, core loop works fully offline

interfaces.name: Outcome
interfaces.signature: enum Outcome: String, Codable, CaseIterable { case completed, stoppedEarly, interrupted, expiredWhileAway, canceledQuickly }
interfaces.notes: Canonical five-value session taxonomy used everywhere.

interfaces.name: LearningState
interfaces.signature: public enum LearningState: String, Codable, Sendable { case defaultState, learning, adapted }
interfaces.notes: adapted requires >=5 non-degenerate completed sessions.

interfaces.name: TimeBucket
interfaces.signature: public enum TimeBucket: String, Codable, Sendable { case earlyMorning, morning, afternoon, evening, night }
interfaces.notes: Computed once from Calendar.current hour at session start; stored, never recomputed retroactively from the raw timestamp.

interfaces.name: SessionProvenance
interfaces.signature: enum SessionProvenance: String, Codable { case userConfirmed, autoReconciled, autoReconciledThenEdited }
interfaces.notes: Explains how final outcome was established.

interfaces.name: SessionRecord
interfaces.signature: @Model public final class SessionRecord { public var id: UUID; public var profileID: UUID; public var startTimestamp: Date; public var plannedFocusDuration: TimeInterval; public var plannedBreakDuration: TimeInterval; public var recommendedFocusDuration: TimeInterval; public var recommendedBreakDuration: TimeInterval; public var recommendationExplanation: String?; public var actualEndTimestamp: Date?; public var actualElapsedSeconds: TimeInterval?; public var outcome: SessionOutcome?; public var wasOverride: Bool; public var energyRating: Int?; public var timeBucket: TimeBucket; public var provenance: OutcomeProvenance }
interfaces.notes: No separate RecommendationSnapshot entity; snapshot-at-start fields folded directly onto SessionRecord.

interfaces.name: Profile
interfaces.signature: @Model public final class Profile { public var id: UUID; public var name: String; public var createdAt: Date; public var isDefault: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Exactly one auto-created default instance in free tier; multi-profile is paid-gated at the view layer only.

interfaces.name: RecommendationEvidence
interfaces.signature: struct RecommendationEvidence: Codable, Sendable { let recentCompletionRate: Double; let recentEarlyStopRate: Double; let overrideCompletionRate: Double?; let dominantTimeBucket: TimeBucket?; let sampleSize: Int }
interfaces.notes: Compact explanation payload for UI and paywall preview.

interfaces.name: Recommendation
interfaces.signature: public struct Recommendation: Sendable { public let focusDuration: TimeInterval; public let breakDuration: TimeInterval; public let learningState: LearningState; public let explanation: String?; public let evidence: [EvidenceMark]; public let progressTowardAdaptation: (completed: Int, needed: Int) }
interfaces.notes: Returned by RecommendationEngine.recommend; cached on SessionCoordinator, never recomputed inside a SwiftUI view body.

interfaces.name: RecommendationEngine
interfaces.signature: public enum RecommendationEngine { public static func recommend(sessions: [SessionSnapshot], previousRecommendation: Recommendation?, tier: SubscriptionTier, now: Date) -> Recommendation }
interfaces.notes: Pure, Foundation-only, no SwiftData/SwiftUI imports. Applies clamps (10-90 min focus, 3-20 min break), step cap (min 10%/5min per recalculation), 60s degenerate floor exclusion, rolling window (20 free / unbounded subscribed), 5-session adapted threshold.

interfaces.name: SessionPhase
interfaces.signature: public enum SessionPhase { case idle(Recommendation), running(SessionRecord), onBreak(SessionRecord), awaitingOutcome(SessionRecord), reconciliationPending(SessionRecord, provisional: SessionOutcome) }
interfaces.notes: Every screen renders as a pure function of this; active-session view binds via fullScreenCover(item:), never NavigationStack push.

interfaces.name: SessionRepository
interfaces.signature: public protocol SessionRepository { func fetchSessions(profileID: UUID, limit: Int?) throws -> [SessionRecord]; func fetchUnresolvedSession(profileID: UUID) throws -> SessionRecord?; func save(_ session: SessionRecord) throws; func fetchProfiles() throws -> [Profile]; func createProfile(name: String) throws -> Profile; func deleteAllData() throws }
interfaces.notes: SwiftData-backed concrete impl is MainActor-bound; sole write path -- HistoryViewModel/@Query is never used directly against SessionRecord.

interfaces.name: NotificationScheduler
interfaces.signature: public protocol NotificationScheduler { func requestAuthorizationIfNeeded() async -> Bool; var isAuthorized: Bool { get async }; func scheduleSessionEnd(sessionID: UUID, fireDate: Date, kind: NotificationKind) async; func cancel(sessionID: UUID) async }
interfaces.notes: Cancels by stable per-session UUID identifier, never removeAllPendingNotificationRequests(). requestAuthorizationIfNeeded checks current settings first; never re-prompts after denial.

interfaces.name: SubscriptionStore
interfaces.signature: @MainActor @Observable public final class SubscriptionStore { public private(set) var entitlementState: EntitlementState; public func purchase(_ product: Product) async throws; public func restore() async throws; public func refreshEntitlements() async }
interfaces.notes: Entitlement checks only gate feature-layer views (profiles, insights); never sit inside SessionCoordinator.start().

interfaces.name: Clock
interfaces.signature: public protocol Clock { var now: Date { get } }
interfaces.notes: Test seam injected into SessionCoordinator so reconciliation/recommendation timing is deterministic in tests.

interfaces.name: SessionCoordinator
interfaces.signature: @MainActor @Observable public final class SessionCoordinator { public private(set) var phase: SessionPhase; public func start(overrideDuration: TimeInterval?); public func stop(); public func skipBreak(); public func confirmOutcome(_ outcome: SessionOutcome, energyRating: Int?); public func reconcileOnLaunch() }
interfaces.notes: Sole writer of SessionPhase. Timer truth = startTimestamp + plannedDuration recomputed from Date(), never a paused in-memory counter. stop()/natural-completion immediately call ReconciliationPolicy.provisionalOutcome(trigger: .manualStop/.naturalCompletion, ...) and persist before the outcome sheet appears; reconcileOnLaunch() uses .appRelaunchReconciliation.

interfaces.name: reconcileOutcome
interfaces.signature: func reconcileOutcome(for session: SessionRecord, now: Date, completionThreshold: Double = 0.9, degenerateFloorSeconds: Int = 60) -> Outcome
interfaces.notes: Shared pure rule used on relaunch and provisional terminal write.

interfaces.name: makePaywallPreviewRecommendation
interfaces.signature: public func makePaywallPreviewRecommendation(from sessions: [SessionSnapshot], tier: SubscriptionTier, now: Date) -> Recommendation?
interfaces.notes: Uses RecommendationEngine.recommend against the user's own real data for the paywall's live preview; view-layer entitlement gate decides visibility, never a stock/fake sample.

interfaces.name: verifyNoNetworkInCorePath
interfaces.signature: public func verifyNoNetworkInCorePath() throws
interfaces.notes: Mechanical structural test enforcing zero-network rule for the timer/logging/adaptation module boundary.

interfaces.name: SessionOutcome
interfaces.signature: public enum SessionOutcome: String, Codable, Sendable { case completed, stoppedEarly, interrupted, expiredWhileAway, canceledBeforeMeaningfulProgress }
interfaces.notes: Fixed 5-value taxonomy; every terminal SessionRecord has exactly one non-nil value.

interfaces.name: OutcomeProvenance
interfaces.signature: public enum OutcomeProvenance: String, Codable, Sendable { case userConfirmed, autoReconciled, autoReconciledThenEdited }
interfaces.notes: Set by ReconciliationPolicy writes and OutcomeCaptureSheet confirm/correct.

interfaces.name: SubscriptionTier
interfaces.signature: public enum SubscriptionTier: String, Codable, Sendable { case free, subscribed }
interfaces.notes: Determines rolling window size passed into RecommendationEngine (20 sessions free, unbounded subscribed).

interfaces.name: SessionEndTrigger
interfaces.signature: public enum SessionEndTrigger: Sendable { case naturalCompletion, manualStop, appRelaunchReconciliation }
interfaces.notes: Distinguishes foreground stop/completion from relaunch reconciliation; ReconciliationPolicy behavior differs by trigger, not one blind elapsed-fraction formula for all three.

interfaces.name: SessionSnapshot
interfaces.signature: public struct SessionSnapshot: Codable, Sendable { public let id: UUID; public let startTimestamp: Date; public let plannedFocusDuration: TimeInterval; public let actualElapsedSeconds: TimeInterval; public let outcome: SessionOutcome; public let wasOverride: Bool; public let timeBucket: TimeBucket }
interfaces.notes: Plain-data mirror of SessionRecord; the ONLY input type RecommendationEngine accepts. Never pass SwiftData @Model instances into engine code or tests.

interfaces.name: EvidenceMark
interfaces.signature: public struct EvidenceMark: Sendable { public let outcome: SessionOutcome }
interfaces.notes: Backing data for the Canvas-rendered evidence strip; 5-8 most recent non-degenerate sessions.

interfaces.name: AppSettingsRecord
interfaces.signature: @Model public final class AppSettingsRecord { public var id: UUID; public var hasRequestedNotificationPermission: Bool; public var hasShownFirstRunExplainer: Bool; public var storeWasReset: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Singleton row. storeWasReset read once by RootView then cleared after the honest-reset message is shown.

interfaces.name: RepositoryError
interfaces.signature: public enum RepositoryError: Error, Sendable { case storeUnavailable, saveFailed, notFound }
interfaces.notes: storeUnavailable triggers the corrupted-store recovery path at app launch via AppLaunchBootstrap.

interfaces.name: ReconciliationPolicy
interfaces.signature: public enum ReconciliationPolicy { public static let degenerateFloor: TimeInterval; public static let completedThresholdFraction: Double; public static func provisionalOutcome(trigger: SessionEndTrigger, plannedDuration: TimeInterval, elapsed: TimeInterval) -> SessionOutcome }
interfaces.notes: Single shared function for all three end triggers, with trigger-specific behavior -- manualStop never resolves to expiredWhileAway.

interfaces.name: NotificationKind
interfaces.signature: public enum NotificationKind: Sendable { case focusEnd, breakEnd }

interfaces.name: EntitlementState
interfaces.signature: public enum EntitlementState: Sendable { case free, subscribed, lapsed }
interfaces.notes: lapsed preserves data (soft-lock), never triggers deletion.

interfaces.name: HistoryViewModel
interfaces.signature: @MainActor @Observable public final class HistoryViewModel { public private(set) var rows: [SessionRecord]; public var filter: HistoryFilter; public func refresh() }
interfaces.notes: Explicit refresh triggered by SessionCoordinator writes; History screen never uses @Query directly against SessionRecord.

interfaces.name: HistoryFilter
interfaces.signature: public enum HistoryFilter: Sendable { case all, completed, earlyStop, interrupted }
interfaces.notes: Backs the History segmented control.

interfaces.name: AppLaunchBootstrap
interfaces.signature: public enum AppLaunchBootstrap { public static func makeModelContainer() -> (container: ModelContainer, didResetStore: Bool) }
interfaces.notes: Catches ModelContainer init failure, deletes the underlying store file, reinitializes empty; didResetStore flag sets AppSettingsRecord.storeWasReset for the honest reset message.

interfaces.name: EmptyStateView
interfaces.signature: public struct EmptyStateView: View { public init(title: String, message: String, systemImage: String) }
interfaces.notes: Shared across History, Settings, and the store-reset screen instead of bespoke per-screen empty states.

interfaces.name: ErrorStateView
interfaces.signature: public struct ErrorStateView: View { public init(title: String, message: String, retryAction: (() -> Void)?) }
interfaces.notes: Paired with EmptyStateView; used for store-error and purchase-failure surfaces.

interfaces.name: ReduceMotionAnimator
interfaces.signature: public enum ReduceMotionAnimator { public static func animation(_ base: Animation) -> Animation? }
interfaces.notes: Single shared helper every .animation() call site routes through; returns nil (instant) when Reduce Motion is on.

verify_summary: FAILED (compile FAILED for the iOS Simulator)
